title: NucleoMag Blood for DNA purification from blood
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol extracts DNA using the [Nucleomag blood DNA kit](https://www.mn-net.com/us/nucleomag-blood-200-l-for-dna-purification-from-blood-744501.4). Pro-k is added to sample, as well as bead buffer. Supernatant is removed with magnets engaged before a dispense of 200ul of mbl3. After supernatant is again removed, ethanol is dispensed and removed in wash before mbl5 is added to sample. The final product is moved to the elute plate on slot 2. Explanation of complex parameters below: * `Number of Columns`: Specify the number of sample columns to run. * `Use filter tips`: Specify whether this run will use filter tips or regular tips. * `mbl5 Volume`: Specify the mbl5 volume in microliters. * `Magnetic engage height (mm)`: Specify the engage height for the magnets to raise under the base of the magnetic plate. The engage height should be empirically tested. * `P300 Multi-Channel Mount`: Specify which mount (left or right) to host the P300 multi-channel pipette. ---
steps_raw: This protocol extracts DNA using the [Nucleomag blood DNA kit](https://www.mn-net.com/us/nucleomag-blood-200-l-for-dna-purification-from-blood-744501.4). Pro-k is added to sample, as well as bead buffer. Supernatant is removed with magnets engaged before a dispense of 200ul of mbl3. After supernatant is again removed, ethanol is dispensed and removed in wash before mbl5 is added to sample. The final product is moved to the elute plate on slot 2. Explanation of complex parameters below: * `Number of Columns`: Specify the number of sample columns to run. * `Use filter tips`: Specify whether this run will use filter tips or regular tips. * `mbl5 Volume`: Specify the mbl5 volume in microliters. * `Magnetic engage height (mm)`: Specify the engage height for the magnets to raise under the base of the magnetic plate. The engage height should be empirically tested. * `P300 Multi-Channel Mount`: Specify which mount (left or right) to host the P300 multi-channel pipette. ---
Dispense 50uL Proteinase K buffer in each well of 96 well plate; need to mix each well as it is added

Dispense 162.5uL bead buffer in to each well; need to mix each well as it is added. It is important the bead buffer is thoroughly mixed in reservoir prior to addition to each well or the beads will settle to the bottom of the reservoir
Place plate on magnet for 3 minutes
Remove supernatant from all wells without disturbing the beads (may need 2 steps to be able to remove all supernatant)
Place plate back on magnet for 3 minutes
Remove supernatant from all wells without disturbing the beads (may need 2 steps to be able to remove all supernatant
Remove plate from magnet. Add 400uL MBL3 buffer to all wells; may need two steps to accomplish this. Mix each well as buffer is added.
Place plate back on magnet for 3 minutes
Remove supernatant from all wells without disturbing the beads (may need 2 steps to be able to remove all supernatant)
Remove plate from magent. Add 400uL 80% ethanol; mix each well as ethanol is added
Place plate back on magnet for 3 minutes
Remove supernatant from all wells without disturbing the beads (may need 2 steps to be able to remove all supernatant)
Allow plate to airdry on magnet for 20 minutes
Remove plate from magnet. Add 100 or 50uL MBL5 buffer to each well; mix each well as it is added
Place plate on magnet for 3 minutes
Transfer 50 or 100uL from each well in to a final DNA storage plate (do not disturb beads)
